title: achillea erba-rotta subsp. moschata
intro: Achillea erba-rotta subsp. moschata, also known as the musky yarrow, is a subspecies of the yarrow plant, characterized by its distinctive musky or sweet fragrance. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, reducing fever, and relieving menstrual cramps. The health benefits of Achillea erba-rotta subsp. moschata include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
